risk_rationale: The INFORM Consumers Act introduces new requirements for verifying high-volume third-party sellers, which is not fully covered by TikTok's current US e-commerce baseline. This poses a material compliance gap that requires immediate product changes to avoid potential regulatory penalties.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: US onboarding guidance requires identity/business verification documentation (for example government ID, SSN/ITIN/EIN in applicable flows, and business registration records for business sellers).
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Online marketplaces must collect, verify, and disclose specific information about high-volume third-party sellers.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline does not explicitly require the disclosure of specific information about high-volume third-party sellers, as mandated by the INFORM Consumers Act.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Prohibited Products Policy bans illegal/stolen/counterfeit/gray-market/sanctioned and recalled products, among other categories.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Consumers should have a clear mechanism to report suspicious activities.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The current baseline does not explicitly provide a clear mechanism for consumers to report suspicious activities related to third-party sellers.

remediations.title: Implement High-Volume Seller Verification and Disclosure
remediations.description: Develop and integrate a system to collect, verify, and disclose specific information about high-volume third-party sellers to comply with the INFORM Consumers Act.
acceptance_criteria: System collects and verifies required seller information.
Disclosed seller information is accessible to consumers.

remediations.title: Develop Consumer Reporting Mechanism
remediations.description: Create a clear and accessible mechanism for consumers to report suspicious activities related to third-party sellers.
acceptance_criteria: Consumers can easily report suspicious activities.
Reports are logged and actionable by support teams.

business_impact: Failure to comply with the INFORM Consumers Act could result in regulatory penalties and damage to brand reputation, affecting consumer trust and market operations.
recommended_actions: Prioritize the development of seller verification and disclosure systems.
Establish a consumer reporting mechanism for suspicious activities.
Conduct a compliance review to ensure alignment with the INFORM Consumers Act.
